learning_outcomes.description: Understand the theoretical bases of professional development, mentoring and supervision.
Demonstrate the application of professional development, mentoring and supervision in work settings.
Demonstrate ethical and professional conduct in professional development, mentoring and supervision.

assessments.task_description:
Assessment task one requires you to complete three reflective journal entries. These should provide an overview of your learning and engagement with the course materials.

Using the ‘Five Rs’ (Bain et al, 1999), you will undertake a process of thinking critically about your learning, your responses to the learning and how you will take that forward into your professional practice in the future.

You will complete three reflections in total of 400 words each (total word count: 1200).

You must have a range of examples of learning materials including:

  • Lectures
  • Readings
  • Online materials and activities

Reflections are to be written in short paragraphs and in first person. There must be evidence of engagement with:

  • theories introduced in the course
  • engagement with range of course learning materials and literature
  • application of the 5 Rs in reflecting on your learning experience
  • consideration of how to apply the learning from your reflection in your professional practice

Five Rs (adapted from: Bain, Ballantyne, Packer & Mills, 1999)

Reporting: what was the source of the learning?

Responding: what were your feelings towards the issue? what was your initial response?

Relating: how did the learning relate to your past and current personal and or professional experiences? why was it significant in the context of learning about Indigenous education?

Reasoning: what theories are relevant in helping to understand and critically reflect on the issue? why do you think you had the initial response and what factors impacted on this?

Reconstructing: why was this a significant learning experience for you as a teacher? how will you take this learning forward into practice?
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Select one topic related to professional development, mentoring and supervision.

Examples of suitable topics include communities of practice, coaching, mentoring, supervision, action research, adult learning, and reflective practice.

Critically review contemporary literature pertaining to the selected topic to facilitate professional growth. Where appropriate you may refer to current policies and reports in your field of work or imagined future field of work (e.g. Professional standards for teachers).

Your literature review should draw from approximately 8-10 references and include a range of quality sources such as books, book chapters and peer-reviewed journal articles. You should draw on literature beyond that contained in the course readings.

Important Note:

A literature review is a systematic examination and synthesis of existing scholarly research on a specific topic or research question. It serves as a comprehensive survey of the academic conversation surrounding your topic. Rather than simply summarising individual sources, a literature review analyses, compares, and synthesizes different perspectives to reveal patterns, debates, and areas where further research is needed.

assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Assessment Item 3 - Professional Learning Plan Presentation
assessments.due_date:
26/10/2026 - 30/10/2026

All supporting materials (slides/overview) must be submitted by no later than 23/10/2026 @ 2pm

Students sign up for the presentation timeslot via MS Bookings
assessments.task_description:
Topic of assignment should be discussed with lecturer prior to commencement.

Details:

Assessment item three is a real-world simulation that builds on the previous assessments in this course. It is intended to provide a practical opportunity to plan for your own professional learning and pitch this plan to a principal/leader in a live, interactive online oral presentation. You will demonstrate your understanding of professional learning, mentoring, and supervision, making direct connections to your own professional context. You will also demonstrate your understanding of how Science of Learning literature and education policy can inform professional learning in education.

You are required to use scholarly sources (using APA) and refer to education policy, relevant theories and literature to support the development of your professional learning plan.

Supporting Materials (prior to presentations)

Submit up to 6 slides (or a one-page overview, max 300 words, excluding references) to support your pitch. This document should give your audience the key reference points for your plan, covering:

  • Context and relevance of your professional learning goal
  • Your identified professional learning need or gap, and its link to the Australian Professional Standards for Teachers
  • An outline of your plan, including timeline and budget
  • A brief statement of justification; why this should be approved
  • A reference list (APA) must be included as an additional slide or on the reverse of the one-page overview; this is not included in the word/slide count.


The Oral Presentation

The 15-minute session is an interactive oral. Students should aim to speak for no more than 10 minutes presenting their pitch; you will be asked targeted questions throughout (not necessarily reserved for the end of the presentation), focusing on your explanation of key ideas and justification of choices within your proposal. Across the full session, you should be prepared to address the following:

  • Justification for this professional learning plan, including the identified gap or need
  • How this professional learning plan aligns with research and/or education policy
  • Expected professional outcomes and benefits to the school/institution, aligned with the Australian Professional Standards for Teachers
  • Timeline and potential budget requirements
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: As AT3 requires attendance at a pre-booked presentation session, UQ's approved extension provisions do not apply to this assessment item. There is no mechanism for late submission, as the task is delivered live at a fixed time. Students who do not attend their scheduled session will receive a grade of 0 for AT3.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Assessment 3 Oral Presentations — Book Your 15-Minute Timeslot

There is no scheduled lecture this week. Assessment 3 oral presentations will take place between Monday 26 October and Friday 30 October 2026. Students must book a 15-minute presentation timeslot via MS Bookings.

All supporting materials, either up to 6 slides or a one-page overview, must be submitted by Friday 23 October 2026.
